response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with a strong software-craftsmanship background, but his professional C++ experience is minimal and dated (roughly six months at National Instruments in 2007-2008, plus a thesis project), and C++ does not even appear in his skills list. His career since has centered on web and full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. He shows no exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL practices, MQTT, or the listed industrial protocols, which are core to this role rather than nice-to-haves.
strengths: Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic, albeit in DLang
Long track record of event-driven architectures, testing discipline (TDD, CI/CD), and mentoring/teaching
Broad polyglot experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded contexts
concerns: Effectively no recent professional C++/STL experience; last hands-on C++ role ended in 2008
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or OT-protocol background
Career trajectory is full-stack web/product engineering, misaligned with an industrial C++ architect-manager role

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or automation domain experience
Strong systems-level and event-driven engineering fundamentals
